**Total count:** 8 merged pull requests

These are all merged PRs that reference Linear issues which are currently in states other than 'done' (such as canceled, backlog, in_progress, in_review, etc.). This suggests that while the code changes have been merged, the corresponding Linear tickets haven't been updated to reflect completion.
I'll help you find merged pull requests that close Linear issues not in 'done' state. Let me start by exploring what data I can access and then design a comprehensive solution

text: Great! I can see that merged PRs have a `:closes_linear` field. Let me check if this field is present in all PRs and what format it has

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp

text: Perfect! Now I need to get Linear issues that are not in 'done' state and cross-reference them with the PRs. Let me first get Linear issues:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Get all Linear issues to understand the structure
(def all-linear-issues (linear__list_issues {}))
{:total-linear-issues (count all-linear-issues)
 :sample-keys (if (empty? all-linear-issues)
               "No issues"
               (keys (first all-linear-issues)))
 :states-distinct (distinct (map :state all-linear-issues))}"}

text: Now let me get Linear issues that are NOT in 'done' state

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:

(def linear-not-done (filter #(not= "done" (:state %)) all-linear-issues))
{:count-not-done (count linear-not-done)
 :sample-issues (take 3 linear-not-done)}"}

text: Perfect! Now let me create a concise summary as requested. The user asked for "each PR number together with the Linear issue id it closes, and the total count"

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Create the final concise output
(str "Found " (count sorted-report) " merged pull requests that close Linear issues NOT in 'done' state:\n\n"
     (clojure.string/join "\n"
       (map (fn [item]
              (str "PR #" (:pr-number item) " closes Linear issue " (:closes-linear item)))
            sorted-report)))"}
